verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN38 family) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein predominantly resident in the trans-Golgi network. However, it continuously cycles between the TGN and the plasma membrane via secretory vesicles and endocytic recycling — a well-documented itinerary used as a classical TGN-PM cycling marker. The ectodomain is briefly exposed on the cell surface during this cycle before retrieval to the TGN, and surface biotinylation / antibody-feeding assays detect a minority PM pool. This qualifies as dual_localization with a documented PM minority pool, not endomembrane_resident only.
